sections.paragraph:
(root בָּטַח with suff. מִבְטַחִי, pl. מִבְטַחִים (dag. f. impl.) m.

(1) confidence, sure and firm hope, Pro 22:19 hence used of the person and thing on which hope is set, Psa 40:5 65:6 71:5 .

(2) security, Job 18:14, “his security is torn out from his tent,” i.e. he himself being too secure. Pl. Isa 32:18.
